  • East Africa

East Africa

East Africa pairs iconic safari landscapes with primate trekking and coast extensions.

This is the strongest first stop for travelers who want a classic Africa trip shape: wildlife, gorillas or chimpanzees, and smooth multi-country connections.

Regional overview

East Africa remains one of the continent’s highest-demand regions for safari, gorilla trekking, coast add-ons, and premium conservation travel. Kenya and Tanzania lead the classic plains circuits, while Uganda and Rwanda anchor primate-led journeys.

These countries welcome visitors with well-established safari routes, strong guide networks, and logical connections by road or regional flight.

Use this page to

  • Compare the countries in East Africa that are most suitable for visitors.
  • Open a country landing page for practical travel context.
  • Jump onward into attractions, stays, and restaurants from the right country base.

Countries

Countries in East Africa

Every country links to its own landing page with a general overview, featured attractions, tour operators, stays, and restaurants.

East Africa

Uganda

Uganda is one of Africa’s best-value wildlife countries, blending gorilla trekking, chimpanzee tracking, Nile landscapes, and savannah safari parks into one route.

Country guide
View Details

East Africa

Kenya

Kenya remains the clearest first-time safari gateway thanks to strong guide networks, the Maasai Mara, private conservancies, and smooth access from Nairobi.

Country guide
View Details

East Africa

Tanzania

Tanzania delivers some of Africa’s most cinematic safari landscapes, from Serengeti migration scenes to the Ngorongoro Crater and Zanzibar’s coast.

Country guide
View Details

East Africa

Rwanda

Rwanda offers a premium, compact travel experience with gorilla trekking, polished logistics, and Kigali as one of Africa’s easiest arrival cities.

Country guide
View Details

East Africa

Ethiopia

Ethiopia appeals to travelers seeking history, ancient religious sites, mountain scenery, and a very different East African experience from safari-led routes.

Country guide
View Details

Featured attractions

High-interest attractions in East Africa

Uganda

Bwindi Impenetrable National Park

Mountain gorilla trekking in one of Africa’s most powerful forest landscapes.

★ 4.9 (842) From $700
Southwestern Uganda
View Details

Kenya

Maasai Mara

Kenya’s classic safari icon for migration drama, predators, and broad savannah horizons.

★ 4.9 (1,384) From $95
Southwestern Kenya
View Details

Tanzania

Serengeti National Park

Vast migration landscapes, apex predators, and one of Africa’s strongest safari names.

★ 4.9 (1,196) From $110
Northern Tanzania
View Details

Uganda

Murchison Falls National Park

Uganda’s flagship Nile-and-savannah park with boat safaris, game drives, and the dramatic falls.

★ 4.7 (615) From $45
Northern Uganda
View Details

Kenya

Amboseli National Park

Big-elephant country with unforgettable Kilimanjaro backdrops.

★ 4.8 (724) From $70
Southern Kenya
View Details

Tanzania

Zanzibar

A safari-and-coast favorite built around Stone Town, beaches, and slower Indian Ocean pacing.

★ 4.7 (983) From $35
Indian Ocean coast of Tanzania
View Details
